data.description:
# Outsider onboarding: "your first change" tutorial, CONTRIBUTING restructure, README front door

## Why

CONTRIBUTING.md assumes Nava-internal context (the dual-remote checkout layout, feature-branch driver groups); there is no from-zero path for an external contributor. Every surveyed Gerrit-keeping project ships a first-change tutorial, and JGit proves a GitHub-OAuth Gerrit supports a very short one (~250 words). Separately, the README is insider-oriented; the 6/6 convergent dev-tool pattern (ruff/uv/httpx/rich/just/fzf) is: H1 → ≤6 badges → one-sentence tagline → an image by ~line 40 → highlight bullets → install → short quickstart.

## What

- New `docs/your-first-change.md`, 800–1200 words, Go's two-layer structure (one-time setup vs per-change loop), LibreOffice-warm tone:
  1. How review works here (3 sentences): Gerrit not PRs; what the `Verified` and `LLM-Review` votes are; GitHub is a mirror.
  2. One-time setup (~5 min): sign in at rebar.solutions.navateam.com with GitHub OAuth → generate HTTP password → clone (origin = Gerrit for outsiders — no dual-remote assumptions) → install the commit-msg hook via the documented curl, with one sentence demystifying Change-Id.
  3. Your first change: branch from main → commit including the `rebar-ticket:` trailer (rebar-specific beat no exemplar has — explain how an outsider gets/references a ticket, coordinated with the shepherded-patch and DCO stories) → `git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main` with the magic ref demystified → reading a −1 (`BLOCK — finding` vs `BLOCK — coverage-gap` infra veto vs `Verified −1` CI failure + `recheck` for flakes) → `git commit --amend --no-edit` + re-push → submit.
  4. Gotchas table: missing Change-Id; pushed to `main` instead of `refs/for/main`; new commit instead of amend; CI flake.
  5. See-also → CONTRIBUTING.md for the full reference (feature branches, stacks).
- CONTRIBUTING.md restructure: external-contributor path FIRST (leading with the tutorial link), Nava-internal dual-remote and feature-branch mechanics moved after; cross-link the shepherded-patch and DCO sections owned by sibling stories.
- README front-door pass per the convergent pattern: one-sentence tagline naming the differentiator (event-sourced ticket store + Jira reconciler for agent swarms; one git-backed store; library + CLI + MCP); 4 badges (PyPI version, Python versions, CI, license); terminal demo image of the core loop (list → claim → close); 5–8 highlight bullets with the tri-surface explicit; install; a ≤15-line quickstart covering all three surfaces (3-command CLI session, 3-line Python snippet, one-line MCP registration).
- Open decision to record: demo asset form (static screenshot vs gif vs asciinema) — peers show no convergence; only "an image early" is universal.

## Scope

IN: `docs/your-first-change.md` (new), CONTRIBUTING.md restructure, README.md top section, one demo asset.
OUT: video tutorials; Gerrit UI screenshot walkthroughs (text-first, JGit-tier); any change to the review gates themselves; deep-dive docs (already exist under docs/).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/your-first-change.md` exists at 800–1500 words covering the nine-beat convergent sequence (account → credential → clone → hook → branch → commit w/ trailer → push refs/for → respond to review → amend/re-push) plus both bot votes and the gotchas table.
- [ ] The external happy path requires zero Nava-internal knowledge (no dual-remote layout, no driver-group references).
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md leads with the external path and links the tutorial; internal mechanics preserved below, clearly marked as internal.
- [ ] README top matches the pattern: tagline, ≤6 badges, image by ~line 40, all-three-surfaces quickstart in ≤15 lines.
- [ ] Demo-asset form decision recorded on the ticket.
data.title: Outsider onboarding: your-first-change tutorial, CONTRIBUTING restructure, README front door
